title: Founder's Climb
subtitle: the startup journey to product–market fit
goalMeaning: Product–market fit and a sustainable exit. You built something people want.

ladders.name: Product–Market Fit
ladders.meaning: Customers pull the product from your hands — everything gets easier.

ladders.name: Mentorship
ladders.meaning: A wise mentor saves you years of costly mistakes.

ladders.name: Runway Discipline
ladders.meaning: Spend like it's your last dollar; your runway is your lifeline.

ladders.name: Grit
ladders.meaning: The startup that refuses to die usually wins.

ladders.name: Shipping Velocity
ladders.meaning: Ship, learn, repeat — speed is your unfair advantage.

ladders.name: Customer Obsession
ladders.meaning: Fall in love with the problem and the customer, not your solution.

ladders.name: Focus
ladders.meaning: Say no to a hundred good ideas to nail the one that matters.

snakes.name: Vanity Metrics
snakes.meaning: Impressive charts, empty bank. Down you go.

snakes.name: Scope Creep
snakes.meaning: Just one more feature… and the launch slips again.

snakes.name: Premature Scaling
snakes.meaning: You poured fuel on a fire that wasn't lit.

snakes.name: Ignoring Customers
snakes.meaning: You built what nobody asked for.

snakes.name: Burnout
snakes.meaning: You ran the sprint as a marathon and collapsed.

snakes.name: Cash Burn
snakes.meaning: The money ran out before the magic did.

snakes.name: Hubris
snakes.meaning: Success went to your head, right before the fall.

snakes.name: Feature Creep
snakes.meaning: Bloated, late, and beaten by a simpler rival.
